title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: ComplexMap package designed powerful, flexible, species-agnostic. contain hard-coded assumptions Homo sapiens model organism. entire workflow depends one simple principle: identifiers used input lists must match. means package’s functions can support organism identifier type (e.g., Gene Symbols, Entrez IDs, Ensembl IDs, UniProt IDs), long consistent. three key inputs identifiers must consistent: Complex List: list protein/gene members complex want analyze. Functional Gene Sets (GMT): database used enrichment analysis. Reference Complex List: “gold standard” list used benchmarking ComplexMap::evaluateComplexes(). (required benchmarking, functional analysis.) example, complex list uses Gene Symbols, GMT file must also use Gene Symbols. complex list uses Entrez IDs, GMT must use Entrez IDs. vignette demonstrates prepare functional gene sets (GMT) various sources , crucially, handle convert identifiers ensure match input data.
code: # For this tutorial, we will assume our input complex list uses Gene Symbols. myComplexes <- list(   CPLX1 = c("POLR2A", "POLR2B", "POLR2C"),   CPLX2 = c("CDK1", "CCNB1", "CCNB2") )

title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: ComplexMap provides several helper functions obtain GMT files. align package’s philosophy clarity avoiding namespace conflicts, call functions explicitly using package::function() syntax (e.g., ComplexMap::getGmtFromFile()) instead using library(). Let’s explore method, paying close attention identifier type returns.

what: Method 1: From a User-Provided Local File
title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: direct method. GMT file, can load ComplexMap::getGmtFromFile(). First, get path example GMT file included package. file uses Gene Symbols. Load GMT file path. Let’s inspect identifiers: Identifier Match: example GMT file uses Gene Symbols. Since hypothetical myComplexes list also uses Gene Symbols, directly compatible ready analysis.
code: gmtPath <- ComplexMap::getExampleGmt() gmtFromFile <- ComplexMap::getGmtFromFile(gmtPath, verbose = FALSE) # First 5 genes in the first gene set: utils::head(gmtFromFile[[1]], 5) #> [1] "ATF2"  "CHUK"  "IFNG"  "IKBKB" "IL2"

what: Method 2: From the Molecular Signatures Database (MSigDB)
title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: msigdbr package provides powerful --date interface MSigDB collections. ComplexMap::getMsigdbGmt() function simplifies process. Identifier Match: default, msigdbr also returns Gene Symbols. directly compatible myComplexes list.
code: # Fetch the Hallmark gene sets for Human # This requires the `msigdbr` package if (requireNamespace("msigdbr", quietly = TRUE)) {   h_gmt <- ComplexMap::getMsigdbGmt(species = "Homo sapiens", collection = "H")    # Inspect the identifiers   if (length(h_gmt) > 0) {     utils::head(h_gmt[[1]], 5)   } } #> Fetching MSigDB sets (Species: Homo sapiens, Cat: H) #> [1] "ABCA1" "ABCB8" "ACAA2" "ACADL" "ACADM"

what: Method 3: From Gene Ontology (GO) via Bioconductor
title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: Using official Bioconductor annotation packages highly reproducible way get functional annotations. databases, however, typically use stable database identifiers, gene symbols. Identifier Mismatch! getGoGmt function returns list genes Entrez IDs (e.g., “5594”, “5595”). match Gene Symbols myComplexes list (e.g., “POLR2A”). Solution: Convert Complex List Identifiers recommended approach convert input complex identifiers match stable IDs annotation database. AnnotationDbi::mapIds function perfect . Now, myComplexesEntrez list directly compatible goGmt generated Bioconductor.
code: # This requires an organism annotation package, e.g., org.Hs.eg.db for human if (requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Fetch Biological Process (BP) terms   # We pass the database object directly using the `::` operator   goGmt <- ComplexMap::getGoGmt(speciesDb = org.Hs.eg.db::org.Hs.eg.db,                                  ontology = "BP",                                 verbose = FALSE)    # Inspect the identifiers   if (length(goGmt) > 0) {     utils::head(goGmt[[1]], 5)   } } #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> [1] "1291" "1738" "1743" "2805" "2806" if (requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Get all unique symbols from our complex list   allSymbols <- unique(unlist(myComplexes))    # Map symbols to Entrez IDs, taking the first ID if multiple exist   symbolToEntrez <- AnnotationDbi::mapIds(       org.Hs.eg.db::org.Hs.eg.db,       keys = allSymbols,       keytype = "SYMBOL",       column = "ENTREZID",       multiVals = "first"   )      # Remove any symbols that could not be mapped   symbolToEntrez <- symbolToEntrez[!is.na(symbolToEntrez)]    # Now, create a new complex list with Entrez IDs   myComplexesEntrez <- lapply(myComplexes, function(complex) {     # Look up the Entrez ID for each symbol     entrez_ids <- symbolToEntrez[complex]     # Return only the successfully mapped IDs, removing any NAs     unname(entrez_ids[!is.na(entrez_ids)])   })    # Inspect the result   print(myComplexesEntrez) } #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> $CPLX1 #> [1] "5430" "5431" "5432" #>  #> $CPLX2 #> [1] "983"  "891"  "9133"

what: Method 4: From Reactome Pathways via Bioconductor
title: 1. Preparing Your Input Data
text: Similarly, reactome.db package provides pathway annotations, also use Entrez IDs. Identifier Mismatch! Like GO example, reactome.db provides Entrez IDs. Solution: solution Gene Ontology. use myComplexesEntrez list created previous step, identifiers match identifiers reactomeGmt.
code: if (requireNamespace("reactome.db", quietly = TRUE) &&      requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE) &&     exists("myComplexesEntrez")) { # Check if conversion above succeeded          reactomeGmt <- ComplexMap::getReactomeGmt(       speciesDb = org.Hs.eg.db::org.Hs.eg.db,       verbose = FALSE   )    # Inspect the identifiers   if (length(reactomeGmt) > 0) {     utils::head(reactomeGmt[[1]], 5)   } } #> [1] "1"     "10019" "10112" "10125" "10125"

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: Protein complexes functional machinery cell. High-throughput experimental methods can identify hundreds putative protein complexes single experiment. ComplexMap package provides comprehensive, end--end workflow process, analyze, annotate, visualize datasets. version 2.0 workflow built around Physical-First philosophy: prioritize physical composition (protein identity) complexes define map’s layout, using functional enrichment provide “color landscape” highlights biological diversity without collapsing distinct sub-complexes generic blobs.

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: ComplexMap package includes demoComplexes dataset (622 putative human complexes). also provide helper locate example BioCarta gene set (GMT) file.
code: # Load example complexes data("demoComplexes", package = "ComplexMap")  # Access the included example GMT file (BioCarta) gmtPath <- getExampleGmt() biocartaGmt <- getGmtFromFile(gmtPath, verbose = FALSE)  # Preview the input message(sprintf("Loaded %d complexes and %d gene sets.",                  length(demoComplexes), length(biocartaGmt))) #> Loaded 622 complexes and 292 gene sets.

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: core package createComplexMap() function. high-level wrapper handles: Refinement: (Optional) Merges highly redundant complexes clean input. Functional Enrichment: Calculates p-values Fold Enrichment every complex. Network Construction: Links complexes based shared proteins (alpha=0.75 gives 75% weight physical overlap). Semantic Clustering: Groups functional terms assign consistent color palette maximizes diversity. Topology Calculation: Runs Fruchterman-Reingold layout calculates centrality metrics (betweenness degree).
code: # Run the entire workflow # We use a subset of 200 complexes for this vignette demonstration cmap <- createComplexMap(   complexList = demoComplexes[1:200],   gmt = biocartaGmt,   ifRefineCpx = TRUE,    # Clean up redundancy   mergeThreshold = 0.90, # Only merge nearly identical complexes   alpha = 0.75,          # Layout priority: 75% Physical, 25% Functional   verbose = TRUE ) #> --- Starting ComplexMap Workflow --- #> Parameters: similarity='jaccard', alpha=0.75 (Diversity Priority) #> Refinement: Enabled #>  #> Step 1: Refining complex list... #>  #> --- Refining Input Complex List (Minimal Merging Strategy) --- #> Filtered 34 complexes by size. Retaining 166. #> Identifying merge groups: method='jaccard', threshold >= 0.90... #> Found 0 redundancy groups. Merging 166 complexes into 166. #>  #> --- Refinement Complete --- #>  #> Step 2: Running enrichment analysis... #> Running enrichment for 166 complexes (Cutoff: 0.05)... #> Annotation complete. Found significant terms for 64 complexes. #>  #> Step 3: Building complex network... #> Building complex network (combined mode, alpha=0.75)... #> Processing 13695 pairs using 11 cores... #> Network built: 94 edges retained. #>  #> Step 4: Generating node attributes... #> Generating node attributes (prioritizing functional specificity)... #>     -> Clustering 135 terms using co-occurrence (jaccard) #> Metric: 'jaccard' with unit: 'log'; comparing: 135 vectors #>     -> Generating diverse palette: 25 functional domains (Average Linkage). #>  #> Step 5: Computing map topology... #> Computing map topology (layout and centrality)... #> Topology computation complete. #>  #> --- ComplexMap Workflow Complete ---

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: One key features ComplexMap object print method, provides immediate feedback “health” landscape. look : Diversity: high count distinct functional domains indicates specific, high-resolution map. Coverage: Shows percentage experimental data biologically annotated.
code: # Inspect the resulting object print(cmap) #> # ComplexMap Object (Physical-First Layout) #> # -- Physical Structure: 166 nodes, 94 edges (0.57 edges/node) #> # -- Functional Landscape: #> #    * Diversity: 24 distinct functional domains (colors) #> #    * Coverage:  38.6% of complexes annotated #> # -- Accessors: `getNodeTable()`, `getEdgeTable()` #> # -- Explore:   `explore(cmap)` to launch interactive Cytoscape.js viewer.

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: can interact map data using standard accessors targeted queryMap tool.

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: Let’s find complexes containing “SMAD4” (central mediator TGF-beta signaling).
code: # Search for protein members smad_complexes <- queryMap(cmap, query = "SMAD4", type = "protein") #> Warning: No matches for 'SMAD4'.  if (nrow(smad_complexes) > 0) {   smad_complexes %>%     select(complexId, primaryFunctionalDomain, proteinCount) %>%     knitr::kable() } else {   message("SMAD4 not found in this subset.") } #> SMAD4 not found in this subset.

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: node edge tables available tibbles custom downstream analysis (e.g., ggplot2).
code: nodes <- getNodeTable(cmap) edges <- getEdgeTable(cmap)  # Example: Top 3 most central complexes by betweenness nodes %>%   select(complexId, primaryFunctionalDomain, betweenness) %>%   arrange(desc(betweenness)) %>%   head(3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   complexId   primaryFunctionalDomain     betweenness #>   <chr>       <chr>                             <dbl> #> 1 CpxMap_0090 BIOCARTA_SALMONELLA_PATHWAY      0.0434 #> 2 CpxMap_0121 BIOCARTA_HBX_PATHWAY             0.0344 #> 3 CpxMap_0088 BIOCARTA_HBX_PATHWAY             0.0212

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: ComplexMap >= 2.0.0, static R plots replaced powerful interactive engine based Cytoscape.js. launch explorer, simply call explore() object. open Shiny application can: Zoom Pan physical landscape. Filter functional domain protein count. Inspect specific nodes see full protein list enrichment scores.
code: # Launch the interactive viewer explore(cmap)

title: 2. Start a Typical Workflow
text: prefer perform advanced network analysis Cytoscape Desktop Gephi, use exportNetwork. sanitizes complex R data types strings writes standard TSV GraphML files.
code: # Export for Cytoscape Desktop exportNetwork(cmap, filePrefix = "my_complex_landscape", format = "cytoscape")

what: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: ComplexMap systems biology R package analysis protein complex landscapes. transforms raw lists putative complexes interactive, functionally annotated maps preserve delicate balance physical composition biological hierarchy.

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: Physical-First Architecture: Layouts driven primarily Physical Composition (shared proteins), ensuring sub-complexes variants preserved rather collapsed generic functional blobs. Interactive Explorer: Built-Cytoscape.js engine via Shiny. Explore landscape high-performance zooming, filtering, real-time node inspection. Diversity-First Refinement: Defaults conservative Jaccard similarity (threshold 0.90) merge true synonyms keeping distinct biological states separate. Specificity-Driven Annotation: Automatically groups functional terms diverse domains calculates Specificity Scores (-log10(p) * log2(Fold)) prioritize unique labels generic terms. Systems Biology Dashboard: S3 print methods provide immediate feedback health map (Functional Diversity vs. Physical Density). Robust Evaluation: Compare predictions gold standards (e.g., CORUM) using Maximum Matching Ratio (MMR), PPV, Sensitivity.

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: can install stable version ComplexMap GitHub.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("zqzneptune/ComplexMap")

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: example demonstrates complete workflow raw complexes interactive map.
code: library(ComplexMap)  # 1. Load demo complexes and example GMT data("demoComplexes", package = "ComplexMap") gmt_file <- getExampleGmt() gmt <- getGmtFromFile(gmt_file, verbose = FALSE)  # 2. Build the ComplexMap # alpha = 0.75 prioritizes physical structure in the layout cmap <- createComplexMap(   complexList = demoComplexes[1:250],    gmt = gmt,   ifRefineCpx = TRUE,   alpha = 0.75,   verbose = TRUE )  # 3. Inspect the Dashboard cmap #> # ComplexMap Object (Physical-First Layout) #> # -- Physical Structure: 224 nodes, 412 edges (1.84 edges/node) #> # -- Functional Landscape: #> #    * Diversity: 25 distinct functional domains (colors) #> #    * Coverage:  42.5% of complexes annotated #> # -- Accessors: `getNodeTable()`, `getEdgeTable()` #> # -- Explore:   `explore(cmap)` to launch interactive viewer.  # 4. Explore Interactively explore(cmap)

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: queryMap function supports exact matches Complex IDs regex searches protein members.
code: # Find all complexes containing the protein SMAD4 res <- queryMap(cmap, query = "SMAD4", type = "protein")  # View the dominant functions and membership dplyr::select(res, complexId, primaryFunctionalDomain, proteinCount)

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: Export landscape standard formats high-resolution publication figures Cytoscape Desktop Gephi.
code: # Exports node and edge TSVs sanitized for Cytoscape Desktop exportNetwork(cmap, filePrefix = "human_proteome_map", format = "cytoscape")  # Export as GraphML exportNetwork(cmap, filePrefix = "human_proteome_map", format = "graphml")

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data
text: Optimize refinement strategy testing multiple thresholds reference set (e.g., CORUM).
code: data("referenceComplexes", package = "ComplexMap")  bench <- benchmarkParameters(   complexList = demoComplexes[1:100],   referenceComplexes = referenceComplexes,   threshold_range = seq(0.7, 1.0, by = 0.05) )

title: A Toolset for the Functional Analysis and Interactive Visualization of Protein Complex Data

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
text: Evaluates different merging thresholds affect quality diversity resulting complex map.

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
code: benchmarkParameters(   complexList,   referenceComplexes,   threshold_range = seq(0.5, 1, by = 0.05),   similarityMethod = "jaccard",   ... )

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
text: complexList list predicted protein complexes refined. referenceComplexes list reference (gold standard) complexes. threshold_range Numeric vector thresholds test. Defaults `seq(0.5, 1.0, = 0.05)`. similarityMethod metric used merging. Defaults **"jaccard"**. ... Additional arguments passed `refineComplexList` (e.g., minSize).

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
text: `tibble` columns: - `mergeThreshold`: threshold tested. - `NumComplexes`: Number complexes remaining (Diversity metric). - `PPV`: Positive Predictive Value (Precision). - `Sn`: Sensitivity (Recall). - `F1`: Harmonic mean PPV Sn. - `Acc`: Accuracy (Geometric mean PPV Sn). - `MMR`: Maximum Matching Ratio.

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** optimizing `mergeThreshold`, trade-removing redundancy (increasing PPV) losing specific variants (decreasing Sensitivity Diversity). function returns standard metrics (PPV, Sn, Acc, MMR) also adds **NumComplexes**. - **Collapse Warning:** `NumComplexes` drops precipitously two thresholds,   likely crossed point distinct biological variants   merged generic blobs. - **Recommendation:** Choose threshold maximizes F1/MMR maintaining   `NumComplexes` count close expected biological complexity.

title: Benchmark Complex Refinement Parameters — benchmarkParameters
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

what: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
text: Constructs network complexes edges represent similarity.

title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
code: buildComplexNetwork(   complexes,   enrichments,   mode = "combined",   similarityMethod = "jaccard",   alpha = 0.75,   nCores = NULL,   chunkSize = 1000,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
text: complexes named list protein complexes. enrichments named list enrichment results (`runComplexEnrichment`). mode Edge weight mode: "compositional", "functional", "combined". Defaults "combined". similarityMethod metric similarity. Defaults **"jaccard"**. **Warning:** Using "overlap" discouraged tends collapse diverse hierarchies single blobs. alpha Numeric (0-1). Weight given Compositional Similarity "combined" mode. Defaults **0.75** (favoring physical structure). nCores Number cores parallel processing. chunkSize Size processing chunks. verbose Logical.

title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
text: `tibble` network edges `weight` representing   calculated similarity.

title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** generate diverse physically meaningful landscape, function defaults prioritizing **Compositional Similarity** (shared proteins) functional similarity. - **Composition (Protein Identity)** treated "Physical Truth". - **Function (Enrichment)** treated attribute. `alpha` parameter controls balance. new default (0.75) gives 75 (like "Cell Cycle") artificially pulling distinct physical complexes single cluster, still allowing functionally related complexes drift closer unrelated ones.

title: Build a Complex-Complex Interaction Network — buildComplexNetwork
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

what: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
text: Calculates 2D layout coordinates key centrality metrics complex network. function serves final step preparing network data visualization.

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
code: computeMapTopology(nodeAttributes, network, seed = 123, verbose = TRUE)

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
text: nodeAttributes `tibble` `data.frame` containing attributes node (complex). Must contain column node identifiers matches source/target columns `network` data. network `tibble` `data.frame` representing network edges. Must contain columns source, target, edge `weight`. seed Integer seed reproducible layout. Defaults `123`. verbose logical value indicating whether print progress messages. Defaults `TRUE`.

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
text: `tibble` containing original columns `nodeAttributes` plus four new columns: `x`, `y` (layout coordinates), `betweenness`, `degree`. table arranged descending order betweenness degree.

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
text: function takes node attribute table edge list (network) performs following steps: 1.  Constructs `igraph` graph object provided data. 2.  Computes force-directed layout using Fruchterman-Reingold algorithm     via `igraph::layout_with_fr`. Edge weights used influence     layout, pulling strongly connected nodes closer together. 3.  Calculates node centrality metrics: - **Betweenness Centrality:** Measures often node lies       shortest path nodes (normalized). - **Degree Centrality:** number edges connected node. 4.  Merges layout coordinates centrality scores back original     node attribute table.

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

title: Compute Network Topology and Layout Coordinates — computeMapTopology
code: # --- Sample Data --- # 1. Node attributes nodes <- tibble::tibble(   complexId = c("Cpx1", "Cpx2", "Cpx3"),   proteinCount = c(10, 8, 12) )  # 2. Network edges net <- tibble::tibble(   source = c("Cpx1", "Cpx2"),   target = c("Cpx2", "Cpx3"),   weight = c(0.8, 0.6) )  # --- Compute Topology --- masterLayout <- computeMapTopology(nodes, net) #> Computing map topology (layout and centrality)... #> Topology computation complete. print(masterLayout) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   complexId proteinCount      x     y betweenness degree #>   <chr>            <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 Cpx2                 8 1.05    4.15           1      2 #> 2 Cpx1                10 0.0791  3.41           0      1 #> 3 Cpx3                12 2.14    4.97           0      1

what: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
text: high-level wrapper function executes entire `ComplexMap` workflow. takes list protein complexes gene set matrix (GMT) performs refinement, enrichment, network construction, topology calculation.

title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
code: createComplexMap(   complexList,   gmt,   similarityMethod = "jaccard",   alpha = 0.75,   ifRefineCpx = FALSE,   layout_seed = 123,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
text: complexList named list element character vector protein identifiers representing complex. gmt named list element character vector genes. similarityMethod metric used comparing complexes functional terms. Defaults **"jaccard"** penalize size differences maintain diversity. Avoid "overlap" wish see specific pathways distinct generic ones. alpha Numeric (0-1). weight given physical protein composition versus functional similarity network layout. Defaults **0.75** (75% physical, 25% functional). ifRefineCpx Logical. **TRUE**, executes Step 1 refine input complex list (merge redundant complexes). **FALSE**, Step 1 skipped, raw `complexList` used downstream analysis. Defaults **FALSE**. layout_seed Integer seed reproducible layout. Defaults 123. verbose logical value indicating whether print progress messages. ... Additional arguments passed core functions: - `minSize`, `maxSize`, `mergeThreshold` (`refineComplexList` `ifRefineCpx = TRUE`) - `pAdjustMethod`, `pValueCutoff` (`runComplexEnrichment`) - `geneSetDb` (`generateNodeAttributes` semantic clustering)

title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
text: validated `ComplexMap` S3 object.

title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
text: function tuned generate **functionally diverse landscape**. enforces following logic: 1.  **Refinement:** (Optional) Uses Jaccard similarity merge highly redundant complexes,     preserving biological variants (subsets/supersets). Controlled `ifRefineCpx`. 2.  **Enrichment:** Calculates 'Fold Enrichment' prioritize specific biological     functions generic ones. 3.  **Network:** Builds layout primarily based **Physical Composition**     (alpha = 0.75), using functional annotations group related clusters,     collapse .

title: Create a Complete Complex Map Object — createComplexMap
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

what: Calculate a Similarity Score Between Two Sets — .calculateSimilarity
title: Calculate a Similarity Score Between Two Sets — .calculateSimilarity
text: Internal helper compute Jaccard, Overlap, Dice coefficients.

title: Calculate a Similarity Score Between Two Sets — .calculateSimilarity
code: .calculateSimilarity(set1, set2, method = "jaccard")

what: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: Calculates four standard metrics evaluating protein complex predictions: Positive Predictive Value (PPV), Sensitivity (Sn), Accuracy (Acc), Maximum Matching Ratio (MMR).

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
code: evaluateComplexes(   predictedComplexes,   referenceComplexes,   nCores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: predictedComplexes list predicted protein complexes. referenceComplexes list reference (gold standard) complexes. nCores number CPU cores use parallel computation. Defaults one less total number detected cores. verbose logical value indicating whether print progress messages. Defaults `TRUE`.

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: named list containing four numeric values: `PPV`, `Sn`, `Acc`, `MMR`. Returns `NA` metrics either input list empty.

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: function optimized speed calculating shared intersection matrix predicted reference complexes parallel. matrix used basis four metric calculations. - **PPV, Sn, Acc** calculated based confusion matrix   predicted reference complexes, described literature (e.g.,   Zhang et al., 2012). - **MMR** calculated first deriving overlap score matrix,   score predicted complex (P) reference complex (R)   `|P ∩ R|² / (|P| * |R|)`.   [Hungarian algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hungarian_algorithm)   used solve maximum weight bipartite matching problem.   approach based method described Nepusz et al. (2012). parallel computation uses `future.apply::future_lapply` cross-platform compatibility (unlike `parallel::mclapply`, available Windows).

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: Nepusz, T., Yu, H. & Paccanaro, . (2012). Detecting overlapping protein complexes protein-protein interaction networks. *Nature Methods*, 9, 471–472. doi:10.1038/nmeth.1938 Zhang XF, Dai DQ, Ou-Yang L, Wu (2012). Exploring Overlapping Functional Units Various Structure Protein Interaction Networks. *PLOS ONE*, 7(8): e43092. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0043092

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

title: Evaluate Predicted Protein Complexes Against a Reference Set — evaluateComplexes
code: # --- Sample Data --- # Predicted complexes pred1 <- c("A", "B", "C") pred2 <- c("D", "E", "F") pred3 <- c("A", "G", "H") predicted <- list(P1 = pred1, P2 = pred2, P3 = pred3)  # Reference complexes (gold standard) ref1 <- c("A", "B", "C", "X") # Good match for pred1 ref2 <- c("D", "E", "F")     # Perfect match for pred2 ref3 <- c("I", "J", "K")     # Unmatched complex reference <- list(R1 = ref1, R2 = ref2, R3 = ref3)  # --- Evaluation --- # Use 2 cores for the example metrics <- evaluateComplexes(predicted, reference, nCores = 2) #>  #> --- Evaluating Complex Predictions --- #> [1] Calculating intersections using 2 core(s)... #> [2] Calculating PPV, Sensitivity, and Accuracy... #> [3] Calculating Maximum Matching Ratio (MMR)... #>  #> --- Evaluation Complete --- print(metrics) #> $PPV #> [1] 1 #>  #> $Sn #> [1] 0.6 #>  #> $Acc #> [1] 0.7745967 #>  #> $MMR #> [1] 0.5833333 #>

One : - `"cytoscape"`: Writes separate node edge TSV files Cytoscape Desktop. - `"graphml"`: Writes GraphML file (via `igraph`). - `"tsv"`: Writes single node-attribute TSV file. verbose Logical.

title: Export a ComplexMap Network for External Tools — exportNetwork

title: Export a ComplexMap Network for External Tools — exportNetwork
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** verify landscape external tools (e.g., Cytoscape Desktop), function ensures specific calculated attributes correctly formatted: - **`score`**: Specificity Score. - **`primaryFunctionalDomain`**: Specific Label. - **`colorHex`**: calculated blended color. performs "Sanitization" step convert R-specific list columns semi-colon separated strings fills `NA` values ensure safe import.

what: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
text: Creates detailed attribute table complex, emphasizing functional specificity network visualization.

title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
code: generateNodeAttributes(   complexes,   enrichments,   geneSetDb = NULL,   similarityMethod = "jaccard",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
text: complexes named list protein complexes. enrichments named list enrichment results. Must contain 'Fold' column specificity weighting. geneSetDb Optional named list gene sets semantic clustering. similarityMethod Distance method clustering ("jaccard", "overlap", etc.). Defaults "jaccard" penalize size differences. verbose Logical.

title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
text: `tibble` complex attributes.

title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
text: function performs several steps generate rich node attributes: 1.  aggregates enriched terms input `enrichments` list. 2.  calculates **Specificity Score** term using formula:     `-log10(p.adjust) * log2(Fold)`. ensures specific, high-fold     enrichment terms prioritized broad, generic terms. 3.  term-complex matrix built, terms clustered. clustering     uses **Average Linkage** forces higher number clusters     preserve functional diversity (avoiding "monochromatic" maps). 4.  unique color assigned functional domain. 5.  complex, **Primary Functional Domain** assigned     enriched term highest Specificity Score. 6.  unique "blended" color calculated mixing domain colors, weighted     Specificity Score.

title: Generate Node Attributes for a Complex Network — generateNodeAttributes
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

what: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
text: diagnostic tool assess complex size distribution distinguish true redundancy (synonyms) biological hierarchy (subsets).

title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
code: qcComplexList(   complexList,   redundancyThreshold = 0.9,   subsetThreshold = 0.9,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
text: complexList list character vectors (protein complexes). redundancyThreshold Threshold Jaccard similarity warning. Defaults 0.9. subsetThreshold Threshold Simpson coefficient (subset) reporting. Defaults 0.9. verbose Logical.

title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
text: Invisibly returns input `complexList`.

title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** maintain diverse functional landscape, critical distinguish two types similarity: 1.  **Jaccard Similarity (Redundancy):** Complex B nearly identical.     *   *Action:* Merge (using `refineComplexList`). 2.  **Overlap/Simpson Similarity (Hierarchy):** Complex subset Complex B.     *   *Action:* **Keep .** merge. often represent distinct         biological states (e.g., Core Complex vs. Holo-Complex). report calculates metrics guide refinement strategy.

title: Perform Quality Control on a List of Protein Complexes — qcComplexList
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

what: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
text: function filters complexes size merges highly redundant ones.

title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
code: refineComplexList(   complexList,   minSize = 3,   maxSize = 500,   mergeThreshold = 0.9,   similarityMethod = "jaccard",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
text: complexList named list element character vector protein identifiers representing complex. minSize Minimum complex size. Defaults 3. maxSize Maximum complex size. Defaults 500. mergeThreshold Numeric (0-1). Threshold merging. Defaults **0.90** (strict) preserve distinct variants. similarityMethod Metric merging. Defaults **"jaccard"**. Options: * `"jaccard"`: (Default) `Intersection / Union`. Penalizes size differences.   Best preserving specific variants. * `"overlap"`: `Intersection / Min(,B)`. Merges subsets parents.   Use want aggressively reduce data. * `"matching_score"`: `Intersection^2 / (SizeA * SizeB)`. Geometric approach. * `"dice"`: `2 * Intersection / (SizeA + SizeB)`. verbose Logical.

title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
text: list containing: * `refinedComplexes`: final list merged complexes. * `mergeMap`: tibble mapping original IDs final IDs.

title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** preserve functional diversity input landscape, function defaults conservative **Jaccard** similarity high threshold (0.90). * **Trust Input:** assume upstream clustering method identified   biologically relevant variants (e.g., complex vs. without regulatory subunit). * **Minimal Merging:** merge complexes effectively   synonyms (nearly identical composition). discourage using "overlap" "matching_score" step, tend merge sub-complexes parents, destroying specific functional signals wish visualize.

title: Refine a List of Protein Complexes by Size and Redundancy — refineComplexList
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis on a List of Complexes — runComplexEnrichment
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** support generation specific diverse landscape, function calculates returns **Fold Enrichment** **Term Size** addition standard p-values. - **Fold Enrichment** used downstream functions prioritize specific   biological labels generic ones. - **Term Size** (PopHits) allows filter overly broad terms   (e.g., containing > 10

title: Summarize Major Biological Themes in a Complex Map — summarizeThemes
text: complexMapObject `ComplexMap` object. method Community detection method ("louvain", "walktrap", "infomap"). Defaults "louvain". add_to_object Logical. `TRUE` (default), returns `ComplexMap` object `themeId` `themeLabel` columns added node table. `FALSE`, returns summary `tibble` themes. verbose Logical.

title: Summarize Major Biological Themes in a Complex Map — summarizeThemes
text: `add_to_object = TRUE`, modified `ComplexMap` object. `add_to_object = FALSE`, `tibble` columns:   - `themeId`: Cluster ID.   - `themeLabel`: consensus functional label.   - `themePurity`: fraction nodes matching primary label (0-1).   - `nodeCount`: Number complexes.   - `edgeCount`: Number internal edges.

title: Summarize Major Biological Themes in a Complex Map — summarizeThemes
text: **Systems Biology Rationale:** Since network layout driven primarily physical composition (alpha=0.75), communities detected represent **Physical Machines** neighborhoods. function characterizes machines functions. single physical machine might contain complexes slightly different specific labels. reflect , labeling logic : 1.  Identify frequent functional domain cluster. 2.  Calculate **Theme Purity** ( 3.  purity low (< 50     (e.g., "Function / Function B") indicate multi-functionality. default, function adds theme assignments directly node table `ComplexMap` object, making easy visualize query theme.

title: Summarize Major Biological Themes in a Complex Map — summarizeThemes
text: Qingzhou Zhang <zqzneptune@hotmail.com>

title: ComplexMap 2.0.0 (2026-02-19)
text: [Core Logic] Shifted Physical-First landscape philosophy. network layout now defaults alpha = 0.75, giving 75% weight physical protein composition preserve biological specificity sub-complex architecture. [Interactive Layer] Replaced static plotting functions (visualizeMapDirectLabels, visualizeMapWithLegend) high-performance interactive engine based Cytoscape.js Shiny. [Visualization] Added explore() runComplexMapApp() launch interactive viewer. [S3 Object] Overhauled print.ComplexMap method provide “Systems Biology Dashboard” highlighting Functional Diversity, Annotation Coverage, Physical Density. [Clustering] Updated functional clustering generateNodeAttributes use Average Linkage dynamic scaling factor force functional diversity prevent “monochromatic” maps.

title: ComplexMap 2.0.0 (2026-02-19)
text: [Export] Enhanced toCytoscapeJSON coordinate scaling edge pruning optimize browser performance large networks. [Export] exportNetwork now supports GraphML sanitizes R-specific data types Cytoscape Desktop compatibility. [Documentation] Fully documented missing arguments (layout_seed, ...) updated vignettes reflect v2.0 API.

title: ComplexMap 2.0.0 (2026-02-19)
text: [Metrics] Fixed critical indexing error evaluateComplexes Maximum Matching Ratio (MMR) return NA predicted reference lists differed size. [CRAN] Eliminated non-ASCII characters complexmap_S3.R qcComplexList.R ensure cross-platform portability. [Architecture] Fixed corrupt lazy-load database issue ensuring consistent namespace exports.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.2 (2026-02-11)
text: [createComplexMap] Added ifRefineCpx parameter (default: FALSE). Step 1 (Complex Refinement) now conditional, allowing users process raw input lists without automatic merging.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.2 (2026-02-11)
text: [generateNodeAttributes] Enforced strict 1:1 mapping Primary Function Node Color. Removed weighted color blending logic ensure visual consistency across functional landscape. [Logging] Updated verbose messaging explicitly state refinement status workflow.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.1 (2025-12-02)
text: [Parallelism] Refactored evaluateComplexes use future.apply instead parallel::mclapply, enabling reliable multi-core computation Windows. [Workflow] Improved summarizeThemes() accept add_to_object = TRUE, allowing theme metadata stored directly within S3 object.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.1 (2025-12-02)
text: [Dependencies] Moved clue Suggests Imports ensure Hungarian algorithm always available MMR calculation. [UI] Fixed runtime error interactive viewer color.argument null. [Vignettes] Resolved build failures vignette_02-workflow.Rmd caused incorrect internal function calls.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.0 (2025-11-15)
text: [Benchmarking] Introduced benchmarkParameters() systematically optimize mergeThreshold values reference complexes. [Metrics] Added dice matching_score similarity options refineComplexList. [Quantitative] Added support color.argument visualization functions map continuous experimental data node colors.

title: ComplexMap 1.1.0 (2025-11-15)
text: [Defaults] Updated default similarityMethod refineComplexList "matching_score". [Traceability] refineComplexList now returns mergeMap tibble reproducibility. [QC] Overhauled qcComplexList output provide formatted diagnostic report instead raw R output. [Terminology] Renamed "overlap" similarity metric "simpson" scientific accuracy.

title: ComplexMap 1.0.0 (2025-10-10)
text: Initial stable release. Introduced ComplexMap S3 object structured proteomics workflows. Added high-level createComplexMap() wrapper. Implemented core analysis suite: summarizeThemes(), queryMap(), exportNetwork(). Released comprehensive documentation three introductory vignettes.
